---
title: "Edible Plants Dataset Analisis (TidyTuesday)"
author: "Leonardo Cárdenas Peralta, Daniel Enrique Molina Sanglade and Enrique Fernandez Salmeron"
date: "April-May 2026"
output: 
  html_document:
    toc: true
    toc_depth: 2
    theme: cosmo
    highlight: tango
---

# Introduction

## Installing libraries

We start our project by installing the libraries we are going to use for accesing the dataset 

```{Installing packages}
install.packages("rmarkdown")
install.packages("dplyr")
install.packages("ggplot2")
install.packages("pak")
install.packages("janitor")
install.packages("skimr")
install.packages("here")
install.packages("tidymodels")
install.packages("performance")
install.packages("tinytex")
install.packages("readr")

pak::pkg_install("tidyverse")
pak::pkg_install("tidytuesdayR")
pak::pkg_install("kableExtra")

```

```{Installing Tinytex}
tinytex::install_tinytex()
```

```{Loading packages}
library(rmarkdown)
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(tidytuesdayR)
library(tidyverse)
library(janitor)
library(skimr)
library(here)
library(tidymodels)
library(performance)
library(kableExtra)
library(readr)
```

## Downloading the dataset

Now we have to downlaod the dataset to start working with it

```{Option 1 to download dataset}
tuesdata <- tidytuesdayR::tt_load('2026-02-03')

edible_plants <- tuesdata$edible_plants
```

```{Option 2 to download dataset}
edible_plants <- readr::read_csv('https://raw.githubusercontent.com/rfordatascience/tidytuesday/main/data/2026/2026-02-03/edible_plants.csv')
edible_plants
```

```{Option 3 to download dataset (Locally)}
# Used to upload the file to Github

download.file(
  url = 'https://raw.githubusercontent.com/rfordatascience/tidytuesday/main/data/2026/2026-02-03/edible_plants.csv',
  destfile = "Data/edible_plants.csv"
)

# Used to have the dataset locally from Github

edible_plants <- read_csv(here("data", "edible_plants.csv"))
```
